levels.name: Initial
levels.description: Ad-hoc and reactive. No formal processes. Issues addressed as they arise.

levels.name: Defined
levels.description: Basic processes exist but are inconsistently applied. Dependent on individuals.

levels.name: Quantitative
levels.description: Data-driven. KPIs tracked. Management uses metrics to drive decisions.

levels.name: Optimising
levels.description: Continuous improvement culture. Predictive analytics. Proactive excellence.

measures.name: Capability Maturity - ICT Governance
measures.description: Tracks the repeatability of processes associated with the capability and consistency of their outputs.

levels.label: Initial — No Roles and Responsibilities defined for this capability. Ownership is absent and activities are performed ad-hoc.
levels.exit: Define and document the most important Roles and Responsibilities for this capability. Assign at least one named owner.

levels.label: Defined — Roles and Responsibilities drafted and a named owner assigned. Not yet formally approved or consistently followed.
levels.exit: Obtain formal approval of the Roles and Responsibilities document. Confirm the owner accepts accountability and begins performing their responsibilities.

levels.label: Managed — Roles and Responsibilities formally approved and actively performed. Owner is accountable and responsibilities are consistently carried out.
levels.exit: Establish a regular review cycle for the Roles and Responsibilities document. Demonstrate that at least one review has occurred and that outcomes from Risk and Reporting have been considered.

levels.label: Quantitative — Roles and Responsibilities reviewed regularly. Performance is measured and outcomes from Risk and Reporting feed into each review.
levels.exit: Demonstrate that a Risk finding or a KPI gap has directly resulted in an update to the Roles and Responsibilities document, closing the improvement loop.

levels.label: Optimising — Roles and Responsibilities are continuously updated in response to Risk findings and KPI outcomes. The improvement loop is fully closed and embedded in practice.
levels.exit: No exit. This is the target state. Maintain through continuous review and demonstrate ongoing responsiveness to Risk and Reporting inputs.

measures.name: Capability Maturity - ICT Risk
measures.description: Tracks the residual risk score of risks associated with the capability and their mitigation status.

levels.label: Initial — No risks or controls documented against any Role or Responsibility for this capability.
levels.exit: Identify the most important Roles and Responsibilities for this capability and draft at least one risk and one control against each.

levels.label: Defined — Most important Roles and Responsibilities identified. Risks and controls drafted against them but not yet formally assessed.
levels.exit: Formally assess every drafted risk and control. Document the residual risk for each. Identify gaps where controls are missing or ineffective.

levels.label: Managed — All drafted risks and controls formally assessed. Residual risk documented. Treatment gaps and additional controls identified.
levels.exit: Implement all identified additional treatments and controls. Reassess residual risk for each responsibility after implementation.

levels.label: Quantitative — Additional treatments implemented and risks reassessed. Residual risk profile established and tracked.
levels.exit: Confirm all residual risks are within the defined tolerance. Establish a regular reassessment cycle and demonstrate at least one completed cycle.

levels.label: Optimising — All risks within tolerance. Continuous reassessment cycle active. Risk and control findings proactively feed back to Governance to update Roles and Responsibilities.
levels.exit: No exit. This is the target state. Maintain through continuous reassessment and demonstrate that risk findings are actively driving Governance updates.

measures.name: Capability Maturity - ICT Reporting
measures.description: Tracks KPI efficacy, reporting cadence and whether outputs drive informed decisions for the capability.

levels.label: Initial — No KPIs defined for this capability. There is no visibility of whether Roles and Responsibilities are being performed or controls are working.
levels.exit: Define at least one KPI for this capability, grounded in the Roles and Responsibilities and the identified risks. Assign an owner responsible for measuring and reporting it.

levels.label: Defined — KPIs defined based on Roles and Responsibilities and identified risks. Not yet being measured or reported consistently.
levels.exit: Begin measuring all defined KPIs on a regular schedule. Produce at least one formal report showing KPI results and distribute it to relevant stakeholders.

levels.label: Managed — KPIs measured and reported periodically. Results are reviewed but exceptions are not yet formally escalated.
levels.exit: Establish a formal reporting schedule to management. Define escalation thresholds — when a KPI falls below an agreed level, a formal exception is raised and tracked.

levels.label: Quantitative — KPIs reported to management on schedule. Exceptions formally escalated and tracked. Reporting is reliable and consistent.
levels.exit: Demonstrate that a KPI gap or exception has directly triggered a review of the Governance Roles and Responsibilities document, closing the improvement loop.

levels.label: Optimising — KPI trends analysed proactively. Gaps and exceptions consistently drive updates to Governance Roles and Responsibilities. The improvement loop is fully closed.
levels.exit: No exit. This is the target state. Maintain through continuous KPI review and demonstrate ongoing responsiveness to reporting outcomes.

capabilities.name: ICT Asset Management
capabilities.description: Lifecycle management of ICT hardware, software, and cloud assets from procurement through to decommissioning.

capabilities.name: ICT Backup, Response & Recovery Management
capabilities.description: Plans, procedures and capabilities to recover ICT services following a major disruption or disaster.

capabilities.name: ICT Capacity & Performance Management
capabilities.description: Monitoring and planning of ICT resource performance and capacity to meet current and future demands.

capabilities.name: ICT Change Management
capabilities.description: Controlled management of changes to ICT services and infrastructure to minimise risk.

capabilities.name: ICT Data Management
capabilities.description: Management of data quality, classification, storage, retention, and governance to ensure data is accurate, secure, and accessible.

capabilities.name: ICT Governance Management
capabilities.description: Frameworks, policies, and oversight mechanisms that ensure ICT activities align with organisational strategy and compliance obligations.

capabilities.name: ICT Incident Management
capabilities.description: Restoration of normal service operation as quickly as possible following unplanned disruptions.

capabilities.name: ICT Major Incident Management
capabilities.description: Coordinated response to high-impact ICT incidents to minimise business disruption and enable rapid service restoration.

capabilities.name: ICT Third-Party Risk Management
capabilities.description: Assessment and ongoing management of risks introduced by third-party vendors, suppliers, and managed service providers.

capabilities.name: ICT Transformation Management
capabilities.description: Planning and delivery of strategic ICT change programmes that modernise technology, ways of working, and service delivery.

capabilities.name: ICT Systems Acquisition, Development & Maintenance
capabilities.description: Governance of the secure acquisition, development, testing, and ongoing maintenance of ICT systems and applications.

capabilities.name: ICT Vulnerability Management
capabilities.description: Identification, assessment, and remediation of security vulnerabilities across ICT infrastructure.
